response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong full-stack engineering credentials with 20 years of experience and proven ability to work at scale (millions of requests/second, distributed systems), he lacks the critical domain expertise for this principal role. The position requires deep specialization in experimentation platforms, metrics infrastructure, feature flagging, and A/B testing frameworks—areas with no evidence in his resume. At principal level, you need domain mastery, not just general engineering strength.
strengths: 20 years of continuous software development with demonstrated ability to work at massive scale (millions of requests/second, distributed microservices)
Proven cross-functional technical leadership through mentoring, open-source maintenance (GISCollective), and teaching workshops; staff-level engineer already working on infrastructure
Strong polyglot engineer with production experience across languages (JavaScript, TypeScript, D, C/C++, Java); demonstrated ability to select and master the right tool for complex problems
concerns: No demonstrated experience building experimentation platforms, feature-flagging systems, or A/B testing frameworks—the core focus of this principal role
Resume lacks evidence of metrics infrastructure, telemetry work, statistical analysis, or data-driven decision-making, all critical to growth/experimentation systems

Limited visibility into leading large multi-workstream initiatives or cross-team stakeholder alignment compared to IC6 expectations; most roles show individual contribution rather than platform-wide influence

key_factors.factor: Proven engineering at scale across distributed systems
Limited evidence of cross-team platform leadership
